triggers.name: Alcatel TiMOS: Unavailable by ICMP ping
triggers.description: Last three attempts returned timeout. Please check device connectivity.

triggers.name: Alcatel TiMOS: High ICMP ping loss
triggers.expression: min(/Alcatel Timetra TiMOS by SNMP/icmppingloss,5m)>{$ICMP_LOSS_WARN} and min(/Alcatel Timetra TiMOS by SNMP/icmppingloss,5m)<100
triggers.description: ICMP packets loss detected.

triggers.name: Alcatel TiMOS: High ICMP ping response time
triggers.description: Average ICMP response time is too high.

triggers.name: Alcatel TiMOS: High CPU utilization
triggers.description: The CPU utilization is too high. The system might be slow to respond.

triggers.name: Alcatel TiMOS: System name has changed
triggers.expression: last(/Alcatel Timetra TiMOS by SNMP/system.name,#1)<>last(/Alcatel Timetra TiMOS by SNMP/system.name,#2) and length(last(/Alcatel Timetra TiMOS by SNMP/system.name))>0
triggers.description: The name of the system has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.name: Alcatel TiMOS: Operating system description has changed
triggers.expression: last(/Alcatel Timetra TiMOS by SNMP/system.sw.os[sysDescr.0],#1)<>last(/Alcatel Timetra TiMOS by SNMP/system.sw.os[sysDescr.0],#2) and length(last(/Alcatel Timetra TiMOS by SNMP/system.sw.os[sysDescr.0]))>0
triggers.description: The description of the operating system has changed. Possible reasons are that the system has been updated or replaced. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.name: Alcatel TiMOS: High memory utilization
triggers.description: The system is running out of free memory.

triggers.name: Alcatel TiMOS: No SNMP data collection
triggers.description: SNMP is not available for polling. Please check device connectivity and SNMP settings.

triggers.name: Check Point: Unavailable by ICMP ping
triggers.expression: max(/Check Point Next Generation Firewall by SNMP/icmpping,#3)=0
triggers.description: Last three attempts returned timeout. Please check device connectivity.

triggers.name: Check Point: High ICMP ping loss
triggers.expression: min(/Check Point Next Generation Firewall by SNMP/icmppingloss,5m)>{$ICMP_LOSS_WARN} and min(/Check Point Next Generation Firewall by SNMP/icmppingloss,5m)<100
triggers.description: ICMP packet loss detected.

triggers.name: Check Point: High ICMP ping response time
triggers.expression: avg(/Check Point Next Generation Firewall by SNMP/icmppingsec,5m)>{$ICMP_RESPONSE_TIME_WARN}
triggers.description: Average ICMP response time is too high.

triggers.name: Check Point: High CPU utilization
triggers.expression: min(/Check Point Next Generation Firewall by SNMP/system.cpu.util,5m)>{$CPU.UTIL.CRIT}
triggers.description: CPU utilization is too high. The system might be slow to respond.

triggers.name: Check Point: Device has been replaced
triggers.expression:
last(/Check Point Next Generation Firewall by SNMP/system.hw.serialnumber,#1)<>last(/Check Point Next Generation Firewall by SNMP/system.hw.serialnumber,#2) and
length(last(/Check Point Next Generation Firewall by SNMP/system.hw.serialnumber))>0
triggers.description: The device serial number has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.name: Check Point: System name has changed
triggers.expression: last(/Check Point Next Generation Firewall by SNMP/system.name,#1)<>last(/Check Point Next Generation Firewall by SNMP/system.name,#2) and length(last(/Check Point Next Generation Firewall by SNMP/system.name))>0
triggers.description: The name of the system has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.name: Check Point: Device has been restarted
triggers.expression: last(/Check Point Next Generation Firewall by SNMP/system.uptime)<10m
triggers.description: Uptime is less than 10 minutes.

triggers.name: Check Point: High memory utilization
triggers.expression: min(/Check Point Next Generation Firewall by SNMP/vm.memory.util,5m)>{$MEMORY.UTIL.MAX}
triggers.description: The system is running out of free memory.

triggers.name: Check Point: No SNMP data collection
triggers.expression: max(/Check Point Next Generation Firewall by SNMP/zabbix[host,snmp,available],{$SNMP.TIMEOUT})=0
triggers.description: SNMP is not available for polling. Please check device connectivity and SNMP settings.

triggers.name: FortiGate: New available firmware found
triggers.description:
New available firmware versions found to download.

This trigger expression works as follows:
1. It can be triggered if there are one or more available firmware versions.
2. `{$FIRMWARE.UPDATES.CONTROL}=1` - a user can redefine context macro to value - 0. That marks this notification as not important. No new trigger will be fired if new firmware is found.

triggers.name: Juniper: Operating system description has changed
triggers.expression: last(/Juniper by SNMP/system.sw.os[sysDescr.0],#1)<>last(/Juniper by SNMP/system.sw.os[sysDescr.0],#2) and length(last(/Juniper by SNMP/system.sw.os[sysDescr.0]))>0
triggers.description: Operating system description has changed. Possible reasons that system has been updated or replaced. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.name: Juniper: No SNMP data collection
triggers.description: SNMP is not available for polling. Please check device connectivity and SNMP settings.

Ignore notPresent(6)

triggers.name: Mellanox: High ICMP ping response time

triggers.name: Mellanox: No SNMP data collection
triggers.description: SNMP is not available for polling. Please check device connectivity and SNMP settings.

macros.description: Warning threshold of ICMP packet loss in %.
Warning threshold of the average ICMP response time in seconds.
Warning threshold of error packet rate. Can be used with interface name as context.
Used as a threshold in the interface utilization trigger.
Link status trigger will be fired only for interfaces where the context macro equals "1".
This macro is used in LTE modem discovery. It can be overridden on the host.
This macro is used in CAPsMAN AP channel discovery. It can be overridden on the host level.
The LTE modem RSRP minimum value for warning trigger expression.
The LTE modem RSRQ minimum value for warning trigger expression.
The LTE modem RSSI minimum value for warning trigger expression.
The LTE modem SINR minimum value for warning trigger expression.
Used in network interface discovery rule filters.
Ignore `down(2)` administrative status
Used in network interface discovery rule filters.
Used in network interface discovery rule filters.
Used in network interface discovery rule filters.
Used in network interface discovery rule filters.
Used for network interface discovery. Can be overridden on the host or linked template level.
Filters out `loopbacks`, `nulls`, docker `veth` links and `docker0 bridge` by default.
Used for network interface discovery. Can be overridden on the host or linked template level.
Ignore `notPresent(6)`
Used in network interface discovery rule filters.
Used in network interface discovery rule filters.
Time interval for the SNMP availability trigger.

triggers.name: MikroTik: Unavailable by ICMP ping
triggers.description: Last three attempts returned timeout. Please check device connectivity.

triggers.name: MikroTik: High ICMP ping loss
triggers.expression: min(/MikroTik CRS312-4C8XG-RM by SNMP/icmppingloss,5m)>{$ICMP_LOSS_WARN} and min(/MikroTik CRS312-4C8XG-RM by SNMP/icmppingloss,5m)<100
triggers.description: ICMP packets loss detected.

triggers.name: MikroTik: High ICMP ping response time
triggers.description: Average ICMP response time is too high.

triggers.name: MikroTik: Firmware has changed
triggers.expression: last(/MikroTik CRS312-4C8XG-RM by SNMP/system.hw.firmware,#1)<>last(/MikroTik CRS312-4C8XG-RM by SNMP/system.hw.firmware,#2) and length(last(/MikroTik CRS312-4C8XG-RM by SNMP/system.hw.firmware))>0
triggers.description: Firmware version has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.name: MikroTik: Device has been replaced
triggers.expression: last(/MikroTik CRS312-4C8XG-RM by SNMP/system.hw.serialnumber,#1)<>last(/MikroTik CRS312-4C8XG-RM by SNMP/system.hw.serialnumber,#2) and length(last(/MikroTik CRS312-4C8XG-RM by SNMP/system.hw.serialnumber))>0
triggers.description: Device serial number has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.name: MikroTik: System name has changed
triggers.expression: last(/MikroTik CRS312-4C8XG-RM by SNMP/system.name,#1)<>last(/MikroTik CRS312-4C8XG-RM by SNMP/system.name,#2) and length(last(/MikroTik CRS312-4C8XG-RM by SNMP/system.name))>0
triggers.description: The name of the system has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.name: MikroTik: Operating system description has changed
triggers.expression: last(/MikroTik CRS312-4C8XG-RM by SNMP/system.sw.os[mtxrLicVersion.0],#1)<>last(/MikroTik CRS312-4C8XG-RM by SNMP/system.sw.os[mtxrLicVersion.0],#2) and length(last(/MikroTik CRS312-4C8XG-RM by SNMP/system.sw.os[mtxrLicVersion.0]))>0
triggers.description: The description of the operating system has changed. Possible reasons are that the system has been updated or replaced. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.name: MikroTik: High memory utilization
triggers.description: The system is running out of free memory.

triggers.expression: last(/VeloCloud SD-WAN by HTTP/velocloud.orchestrator.build,#1)<>last(/VeloCloud SD-WAN by HTTP/velocloud.orchestrator.build,#2) and length(last(/VeloCloud SD-WAN by HTTP/velocloud.orchestrator.build))>0
triggers.description: The VeloCloud Orchestrator build has changed.

triggers.name: VeloCloud: Orchestrator version has changed
triggers.expression: last(/VeloCloud SD-WAN by HTTP/velocloud.orchestrator.version,#1)<>last(/VeloCloud SD-WAN by HTTP/velocloud.orchestrator.version,#2) and length(last(/VeloCloud SD-WAN by HTTP/velocloud.orchestrator.version))>0
triggers.description: The VeloCloud Orchestrator version has changed.

triggers.name: VeloCloud: System properties have changed
triggers.expression: last(/VeloCloud SD-WAN by HTTP/velocloud.system.properties,#1)<>last(/VeloCloud SD-WAN by HTTP/velocloud.system.properties,#2)
triggers.description: The system properties have changed.

macros.description: VeloCloud SD-WAN Enterprise ID. Specify a single Enterprise ID (requires READ EDGE privileges). By default parameter is empty, data will be retrieved for all Enterprises (privileges required: READ ENTERPRISE and READ EDGE).
The HTTP proxy for script items (set if needed). If the macro is empty, then no proxy is used.
Filter for discoverable Edges by name.
Filter to exclude discovered Edges by name.
Filter for discoverable Edges by state.
Filter to exclude discovered Edges by state.
Filter for discoverable gateways.
Filter to exclude discovered gateways.
Response timeout for API.
Update interval for raw item, expressed in hours.
VeloCloud SD-WAN Orchestrator API token.
VeloCloud SD-WAN Orchestrator URL, e.g., `vco.velocloud.net`.

triggers.name: Vyatta: Unavailable by ICMP ping
triggers.description: Last three attempts returned timeout. Please check device connectivity.

triggers.name: Vyatta: High ICMP ping loss
triggers.expression: min(/Vyatta Virtual Router by SNMP/icmppingloss,5m)>{$VYATTA.ICMP.LOSS.WARN} and min(/Vyatta Virtual Router by SNMP/icmppingloss,5m)<100
triggers.description: ICMP packet loss detected.

triggers.name: Vyatta: High ICMP ping response time
triggers.description: Average ICMP response time is too high.

triggers.name: Vyatta: Memory utilization is high
triggers.description: Memory utilization is high.

triggers.name: Vyatta: Memory utilization is too high
triggers.description: Memory utilization is too high.

triggers.name: Vyatta: No SNMP data collection
triggers.description: SNMP is not available for polling. Please check device connectivity and SNMP settings.

macros.description: High severity threshold of CPU utilization.
Warning threshold of CPU utilization.
Sets the IP regex filter to use in BGP peer discovery for including.
Sets the IP regex filter to use in BGP peer discovery for excluding.
Sets the OID regex filter to use in CPU discovery for including. Matches `hrDeviceProcessor` from MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-TYPES - the device type identifier used for a CPU.
Sets the OID regex filter to use in CPU discovery for excluding. Matches `hrDeviceProcessor` from MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-TYPES - the device type identifier used for a CPU.
Sets the administrative status regex filter to use in network interface discovery for including.
Sets the administrative status regex filter to use in network interface discovery for excluding.
Sets the alias regex filter to use in network interface discovery for including.
Sets the alias regex filter to use in network interface discovery for excluding.
Sets the description regex filter to use in network interface discovery for including.
Sets the description regex filter to use in network interface discovery for excluding.
Sets the name regex filter to use in network interface discovery for including.
Sets the name regex filter to use in network interface discovery for excluding. Filters out `loopbacks`, `nulls`, docker `veth` links, and `docker0 bridge` by default.
Sets the operative status regex filter to use in network interface discovery for including.
Sets the operative status regex filter to use in network interface discovery for excluding. Ignore `notPresent(6)`.
Sets the type regex filter to use in network interface discovery for including.
Sets the type regex filter to use in network interface discovery for excluding.
Sets the name regex filter to use in storage discovery for including.
Sets the name regex filter to use in storage discovery for excluding.
Sets the type regex filter to use in storage discovery for including.
Sets the type regex filter to use in storage discovery for excluding.
Warning threshold of ICMP packet loss in %.
Warning threshold of the average ICMP response time in seconds.
Macro for the operational state of the interface for the link down trigger. Can be used with the interface name as context.
Load per CPU considered sustainable. Tune if needed.
High severity threshold of memory utilization.
Warning threshold of memory utilization.
Time interval for the SNMP availability trigger.
High severity threshold of storage utilization.
Warning threshold of storage utilization.

triggers.name: ZYXEL: No SNMP data collection
triggers.description: SNMP is not available for polling. Please check device connectivity and SNMP settings.

triggers.name: ZYXEL: Firmware has changed
triggers.expression: last(/ZYXEL AAM1212-51 IES-612 by SNMP/zyxel.aam1212.fwversion,#1)<>last(/ZYXEL AAM1212-51 IES-612 by SNMP/zyxel.aam1212.fwversion,#2) and length(last(/ZYXEL AAM1212-51 IES-612 by SNMP/zyxel.aam1212.fwversion))>0
triggers.description: Firmware version has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.name: ZYXEL: Template does not match hardware
triggers.expression: last(/ZYXEL AAM1212-51 IES-612 by SNMP/zyxel.aam1212.model)<>"AAM1212-51 / IES-612"
triggers.description: This template is for Zyxel AAM1212-51 / IES-612, but connected to {ITEM.VALUE}

triggers.name: ZYXEL: Device has been replaced
triggers.expression: last(/ZYXEL AAM1212-51 IES-612 by SNMP/zyxel.aam1212.serialnumber,#1)<>last(/ZYXEL AAM1212-51 IES-612 by SNMP/zyxel.aam1212.serialnumber,#2) and length(last(/ZYXEL AAM1212-51 IES-612 by SNMP/zyxel.aam1212.serialnumber))>0
triggers.description: Device serial number has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.name: ZYXEL: Port {#SNMPINDEX} alarm
triggers.description: The slot reported an error.

triggers.description: SNMP is not available for polling. Please check device connectivity and SNMP settings.

triggers.name: ZYXEL: High CPU utilization
triggers.description: The CPU utilization is too high. The system might be slow to respond.

triggers.name: ZYXEL: Firmware has changed
triggers.expression: last(/ZYXEL XGS-4728F by SNMP/zyxel.4728f.fwversion,#1)<>last(/ZYXEL XGS-4728F by SNMP/zyxel.4728f.fwversion,#2) and length(last(/ZYXEL XGS-4728F by SNMP/zyxel.4728f.fwversion))>0
triggers.description: Firmware version has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.name: ZYXEL: Template does not match hardware
triggers.description: This template is for Zyxel XGS-4728F, but connected to {ITEM.VALUE}

triggers.name: ZYXEL: Device has been replaced
triggers.expression: last(/ZYXEL XGS-4728F by SNMP/zyxel.4728f.serialnumber,#1)<>last(/ZYXEL XGS-4728F by SNMP/zyxel.4728f.serialnumber,#2) and length(last(/ZYXEL XGS-4728F by SNMP/zyxel.4728f.serialnumber))>0
triggers.description: Device serial number has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.
